The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神|12=draft}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|12=draft}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道|12=draft}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|12=draft}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|12=draft}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Tamaki Town|ja|玉城町|12=draft}} Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}. The sacred object ({{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|12=draft}}) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神|12=draft}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|12=draft}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道|12=draft}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|12=draft}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|12=draft}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. revision.comment: Text replacement - "rank" to "Rank"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Tamaki Town|ja|玉城町|12=draft}} Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}. The sacred object ({{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|12=draft}}) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神|12=draft}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|12=draft}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道|12=draft}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|12=draft}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|12=draft}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:摂末社]] to [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}{{ill|Tamaki Town|ja|玉城町}} Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The sacred object ({{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai}}) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

revision.comment: 10 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:朽羅神社 up to rev 98113671
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 朽羅神社 |画像=[[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]原[[小字|字]]森ノ前272 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 1.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 35 | 経度秒 = 1.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 千依比賣命
千依比古命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[皇大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 宮田森 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''朽羅神社'''（くちらじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。内宮の摂社27社のうち第12位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。 [[祭神]]は農耕神で、[[境内]]は水田の中に浮かぶ緑の小島のように見える伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：62, 64ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]原[[小字|字]]森ノ前272に鎮座する辰巳出版（2013）：59ページ。鎮座地は「宮田森」と呼ばれる学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ。[[倭姫命]]の定めた神社である。石を[[神体]]とする金子（1983）：59ページ。 社名の「朽羅」とは、「籠る」（こもる）という意味であり、[[神 (神道)|神]]の籠る森であることを指している伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：64ページ。別の説では、「朽羅」は「口原」であり、道の入り口を指すと解釈する式内社研究会 編（1990）：315ページ。「朽羅」の読みに関しては、「くちら」とする[[史料]]と「くたみ」とする史料がある。 境内の入り口には石をくりぬいた[[手水舎]]があり、くねった[[参道]]を進むと小さな[[社殿]]に達する{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=レディオ キューブ FM三重 |work=8月17日放送 玉城町 朽羅（くちら）神社 |publisher=[[三重エフエム放送]] |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}。社殿は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で長さ6尺（≒1.8[[メートル|m]]）×広さ4尺（≒1.2m）である式内社研究会 編（1990）：317ページ。社地の[[面積]]は3反9畝9歩（≒3,898[[平方キロメートル|m2]]）宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：10ページ。 == 祭神 == 祭神は千依比賣命（ちよりひめのみこと）と千依比古命（ちよりひこのみこと）。どちらも田野の[[守護神]]である。2柱はともに[[年神|大歳神]]の子であり、千依比賣命が姉、千依比古命が弟である。 『神名秘書』では祭神を[[水神]]とする。 == 歴史 == 『皇大神宮儀式帳』には「朽羅神社」の記載はなく、「久麻良比神社」として掲載されている式内社研究会 編（1990）：316ページ。久麻良比神社は千依比賣命と千依比古命を祭神としており、倭姫命が定めた神社であった。 [[寛文]]3年[[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1663年]][[9月30日]]）に再興された。ただし、[[御巫清直]]は再興地が旧社地ではないと述べ、[[江戸時代]]には既に廃村となっていた旧原村の産土神が旧社地であるとした式内社研究会 編（1990）：315 - 316ページ。「朽羅」を「くたみ」と読むと主張する[[伴信友]]は、朽羅神社の読みが[[二見浦|二見]]（ふたみ）に似ていることから、二見に鎮座していた神社ではないかと解釈している式内社研究会 編（1990）：316 - 317ページ。 社殿は[[1908年]]（明治41年）12月に建て替えられている。 == 祭祀 == [[雨乞い]]の[[信仰]]のある神社であり、雨の降らない日が続くと住民が[[鉦]]・[[太鼓]]・[[ほら貝]]を鳴らしながら「アーメ、ターモレ、ターベーヨ」（雨賜もれ給べよ）と復唱した。 == 周辺 == 神社は方角さえ間違えなければ分かりやすい位置に鎮座する{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=コース19 玉城ルート2 〜古薫る田園の神々を巡る〜|publisher=伊勢市観光協会|accessdate=2014-06-04|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}}。 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[外城田駅]]より徒歩約30分（約2[[キロメートル|km]]）。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]から[[自動車]]で約13分（約6.4km）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58年8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[東外城田村]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くちらしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 朽羅神社 |画像=[[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]原[[小字|字]]森ノ前272 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 1.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 35 | 経度秒 = 1.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 千依比賣命
千依比古命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[皇大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 宮田森 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''朽羅神社'''（くちらじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。内宮の摂社27社のうち第12位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。 [[祭神]]は農耕神で、[[境内]]は水田の中に浮かぶ緑の小島のように見える伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：62, 64ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]原[[小字|字]]森ノ前272に鎮座する辰巳出版（2013）：59ページ。鎮座地は「宮田森」と呼ばれる学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ。[[倭姫命]]の定めた神社である。石を[[神体]]とする金子（1983）：59ページ。 社名の「朽羅」とは、「籠る」（こもる）という意味であり、[[神 (神道)|神]]の籠る森であることを指している伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：64ページ。別の説では、「朽羅」は「口原」であり、道の入り口を指すと解釈する式内社研究会 編（1990）：315ページ。「朽羅」の読みに関しては、「くちら」とする[[史料]]と「くたみ」とする史料がある。 境内の入り口には石をくりぬいた[[手水舎]]があり、くねった[[参道]]を進むと小さな[[社殿]]に達する{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=レディオ キューブ FM三重 |work=8月17日放送 玉城町 朽羅（くちら）神社 |publisher=[[三重エフエム放送]] |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}。社殿は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で長さ6尺（≒1.8[[メートル|m]]）×広さ4尺（≒1.2m）である式内社研究会 編（1990）：317ページ。社地の[[面積]]は3反9畝9歩（≒3,898[[平方キロメートル|m2]]）宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：10ページ。 == 祭神 == 祭神は千依比賣命（ちよりひめのみこと）と千依比古命（ちよりひこのみこと）。どちらも田野の[[守護神]]である。2柱はともに[[年神|大歳神]]の子であり、千依比賣命が姉、千依比古命が弟である。 『神名秘書』では祭神を[[水神]]とする。 == 歴史 == 『皇大神宮儀式帳』には「朽羅神社」の記載はなく、「久麻良比神社」として掲載されている式内社研究会 編（1990）：316ページ。久麻良比神社は千依比賣命と千依比古命を祭神としており、倭姫命が定めた神社であった。 [[寛文]]3年[[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1663年]][[9月30日]]）に再興された。ただし、[[御巫清直]]は再興地が旧社地ではないと述べ、[[江戸時代]]には既に廃村となっていた旧原村の産土神が旧社地であるとした式内社研究会 編（1990）：315 - 316ページ。「朽羅」を「くたみ」と読むと主張する[[伴信友]]は、朽羅神社の読みが[[二見浦|二見]]（ふたみ）に似ていることから、二見に鎮座していた神社ではないかと解釈している式内社研究会 編（1990）：316 - 317ページ。 社殿は[[1908年]]（明治41年）12月に建て替えられている。 == 祭祀 == [[雨乞い]]の[[信仰]]のある神社であり、雨の降らない日が続くと住民が[[鉦]]・[[太鼓]]・[[ほら貝]]を鳴らしながら「アーメ、ターモレ、ターベーヨ」（雨賜もれ給べよ）と復唱した。 == 周辺 == 神社は方角さえ間違えなければ分かりやすい位置に鎮座する{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=コース19 玉城ルート2 〜古薫る田園の神々を巡る〜|publisher=伊勢市観光協会|accessdate=2014-06-04|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}}。 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[外城田駅]]より徒歩約30分（約2[[キロメートル|km]]）。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]から[[自動車]]で約13分（約6.4km）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58年8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[東外城田村]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くちらしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}{{ill|Tamaki Town|ja|玉城町}} Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The sacred object ({{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai}}) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: cleanup/reformat
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Tamaki Town|ja|玉城町|12=draft}} Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}. The sacred object ({{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|12=draft}}) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神|12=draft}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|12=draft}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道|12=draft}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|12=draft}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|12=draft}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

The sacred object ({{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft}}) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神|qq=draft}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道|qq=draft}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道|qq=draft}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}{{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft}}{{ill|Tamaki Town|ja|玉城町|qq=draft}} Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft}}. The sacred object ({{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft}}) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神|qq=draft}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道|qq=draft}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:朽羅神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}{{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft}}{{ill|Tamaki Town|ja|玉城町|qq=draft}} Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft}}. The sacred object ({{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft}}) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神|qq=draft}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道|qq=draft}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p.
