ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Shikinai-sha" to "Shikinaisha"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}{{ill|Tamaki Town|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Town|qq=}} Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}. The sacred object ({{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shintai|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}}) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a {{ill|kami (Shinto)|ja|神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami (Shinto)|qq=}} residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=}}. Following the winding {{ill|sando (Shinto)|ja|参道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sando (Shinto)|qq=}}, one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered {{ill|suijin (water deity)|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suijin (water deity)|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban Nobutomo|qq=}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami|qq=}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}} {{ill|Sotojida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotojida Station|qq=}}: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}} {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]]

Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=}}. The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban Nobutomo|qq=}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami|qq=}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]][[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]] Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The sacred object ([[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a [[:en:Deity|kami (Shinto)]] residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=}}. Following the winding [[:en:Sandō|sando (Shinto)]], one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban Nobutomo|qq=}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami|qq=}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]]

The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: ill fix final-12
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]][[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]] Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The sacred object ([[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a [[:en:Deity|kami (Shinto)]] residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}}. Following the winding [[:en:Sandō|sando (Shinto)]], one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban Nobutomo|qq=}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami|qq=}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]]

ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== 参考文献 ==" to "== Bibliography =="
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]][[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]] Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The sacred object ([[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a [[:en:Deity|kami (Shinto)]] residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}}. Following the winding [[:en:Sandō|sando (Shinto)]], one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban Nobutomo|qq=}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami|qq=}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]]

It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]][[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]] Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The sacred object ([[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a [[:en:Deity|kami (Shinto)]] residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}}. Following the winding [[:en:Sandō|sando (Shinto)]], one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban Nobutomo|qq=}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami|qq=}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城の Municipal History]]

The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]][[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]] Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The sacred object ([[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a [[:en:Deity|kami (Shinto)]] residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}}. Following the winding [[:en:Sandō|sando (Shinto)]], one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban Nobutomo|qq=}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami|qq=}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]]

ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Shikinaisha]]" to "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}}"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]][[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]] Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The sacred object ([[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a [[:en:Deity|kami (Shinto)]] residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}}. Following the winding [[:en:Sandō|sando (Shinto)]], one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban Nobutomo|qq=}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami|qq=}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229533]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | Name = Kuchira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kuchira-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]], Hara Aza Morinomae 272 | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 29 | lat_sec = 1.9 | lon_deg = 136 | lon_min = 35 | lon_sec = 1.5 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Chiyori-hime no Mikoto (千依比賣命)
Chiyori-hiko no Mikoto (千依比古命) | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (摂社) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Miyatamori (宮田森) | Festivals = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (神嘗祭) }} '''Kuchira Shrine''' (朽羅神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 12th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of Kōtai Jingū.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The enshrined deities are agricultural gods, and the shrine grounds appear like a small green island floating amidst rice paddies.Compiled by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 62, 64. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]][[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki Town]] Hara, Mori-no-mae 272Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.59. The site of the shrine is known as "Miyata Mori"Gakken Publishing (2013): p.68. It is a shrine established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The sacred object ([[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. The name "Kuchira" means "to enshrine" or "to confine," referring to a forest where a [[:en:Deity|kami (Shinto)]] residesIse Bunka Publishing (2008): p.64. Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}}. Following the winding [[:en:Sandō|sando (Shinto)]], one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. The shrine grounds cover approximately 3,898 m2Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10. == Enshrined Kami == The enshrined deities are Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother. According to "Jinmei Hisyo," the kami are considered [[:en:Suijin|water god]]. == History == The shrine is not listed as "Kuchira Shrine" in the "Kotaijingu Gishikicho," but appears as "Kumarabi Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.316. Kumarabi Shrine enshrined Chiyorihime-no-Mikoto and Chiyorihiko-no-Mikoto, established by Yamato-hime-no-mikoto. The shrine was restored on August 29, Kanbun 3 (Gregorian date: September 30, 1663). However, {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} claimed that the restored site was not the original, suggesting that the ancestral god of the now-abandoned Hara Village was the original siteShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 315-316. {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ban Nobutomo|qq=}} argued for the reading "Kutami," speculating that it was originally located in {{ill|Futami|ja|二見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami|qq=}} due to phonetic similaritiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 316-317. The shrine was rebuilt in December 1908 (Meiji 41). == Rituals == The shrine is known for rituals praying for rain, where residents beat gongs, drums, and conch shells while chanting "A-me, Ta-more, Ta-beyo" (meaning "grant us rain") when droughts persist. == Surroundings == The shrine is easily located if the direction is correct{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 ~ Touring the Ancient Deities of the Countryside ~ |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2014-06-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026235527/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojida Station]]: about 30 minutes on foot (approx. 2 km). * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|qq=}}: about 13 minutes by car (approx. 6.4 km). == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_04.jpg 朽羅神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0702.html 朽羅神社（くちらじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * {{Wayback |url=http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/hpdata/kanbun/jinjya.html |title=神社めぐり |date=20100614012409}}：玉城町 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|朽羅神社|version=98113671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:朽羅神社]] [[sn:Kuchira Shrine]] [[sn:uchira Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Another theory interprets "Kuchira" as "Kuchihara," meaning the entrance to a pathShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.315. The reading of "Kuchira" varies between "Kuchira" and "Kutami" depending on historical records. At the entrance of the precincts is a stone-carved {{ill|temizuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temizuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}}. Following the winding [[:en:Sandō|sando (Shinto)]], one reaches a small {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |title=Radio Cube FM Mie |work=Broadcast on August 17, Tamaki Town Kuchira Shrine |publisher=Mie FM Broadcasting |date=2013-04-04 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140604070523/http://www.fmmie.jp/diary/program/okage/index.cgi?no=51 |archivedate=2014-06-04 |accessdate=2014-06-04}}. The shrine is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, measuring 6 shaku (approx. 1.8 m) in length and 4 shaku (approx. 1.2 m) in widthShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.317. Both are guardian deities of rice fields. They are siblings, children of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, with Chiyorihime being the elder sister and Chiyorihiko the younger brother.
